figures.caption: Figure 1. Comparison of discriminative, SDE-based and our IRFlow paradigms that bridges generative and discriminative image restoration.
Table 1. Quantitative comparison on two deraining datasets.
Table 2. Quantitative comparison on three denoising datasets with σ = 25.
Figure 15. More visual results between 1-Rectified Flow standard velocity and our velocity on CIFAR10 (32 × 32) dataset.
Figure 2. Overall framework of IR-Flow. The approach achieves image restoration through Rectified Flow, refined by cumulative velocity field and multi-step consistency training.
Figure 6. Training and Performance curves of the IR-Flow on progressive optimization methods.
Figure 3. Comparison of two velocity fields. Standard velocity fields represents the instantaneous tangent directions. Our representation captures accumulated velocity toward the target state.

Figure 10. Multi-step solution results on denoising. As the number of solving steps increases, the details and textures of the recovery results gradually become richer. (Zoom in visualization for detailed inspection of local structures. For example: fur, texture, etc.)
Figure 7. Visual results of our IR-Flow method of dehazing on the RESIDE-6k [43] dataset, 4× Super-Resolution on the DIV2K [2] dataset and real-world image denoising on SIDD [1] dataset.
Figure 12. More visual results on Rainy dataset.
Figure 11. Visual results of the model on the real world deblurring GoPro dataset.
Figure 9. Multi-step solution results on denoising. As the number of solving steps increases, the details and textures of the recovery results gradually become richer.

Figure 8. Visual results of the model trained on Rain100H evaluated on the Rain100L and real-world Internet dataset for out-of-distribution (OOD) analysis. Our approach achieves higher quantitative metrics while producing cleaner rain-removal results.
Figure 14. Qualitative comparison results on Raindrop dataset.
Figure 13. More visual results on Noisy dataset.
Figure 4. Visual results of IR-Flow and competing approaches. The inference steps are set to 2 for deraining and raindrop removal, and 4 for denoising.
Table 16. Quantitative results of image denoising on CBSD68 [38] test set.
Table 15. Quantitative results of image denoising on Kodak24 [14] test set.
Table 14. Quantitative results of image denoising on McMaster [73] test set. Our method achieves state-of-the-art distortion and perceptual metrics in 4 steps. P: PSNR, S: SSIM, L: LPIPS, F: FID.
